items.title: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Josh Minott to a two-year, $9 million deal. Team option Year 2. He averaged 10.8 points on 49% shooting from the field and 40% from 3-point range in 19.3 minutes for Brooklyn last season. Agent Aaron Turner and Nets officials did the deal.

more than 6.5 offensive rebounds and 
more than 2 blocks per-36 minutes. 

In 2025-26, 
Mitchell Robinson averaged:
 7.7 (!!) offensive rebounds and
2.1 blocks per-36 minutes

items.title: Here's what the Warriors are working with (factoring in Al Horford's new deal, plus Yaxel Lendeborg):
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Here's what the Warriors are working with (factoring in Al Horford's new deal, plus Yaxel Lendeborg):

$43.7M under the luxury tax
$51.7M under the first apron

Should have the $15M NTMLE, plus lots of flexibility to take on salary in trades. Keep an eye on the first apron being a likely hard cap.

items.title: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.

June 29, 1992: The U.S. men's Olympic basketball team defeats Canada, 105-61, during the preliminary round of the Tournament of the Americas in Portland.

Charles Barkley scored 19 PTS and Karl Malone had 15 PTS for the "Dream Team." Mike Smrek had 14 PTS for Canada.

📺 ESPN/CNN

items.title: The Sacramento Kings have to sort through ways to shed salary this summer. The Kings are currently $12.1M over the luxury tax, $4.1M over the first apron and $8.8M under the second apron.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
The Sacramento Kings have to sort through ways to shed salary this summer. The Kings are currently $12.1M over the luxury tax, $4.1M over the first apron and $8.8M under the second apron.

Easiest path to savings is waiving DeMar DeRozan, but expect Sacramento to look for trades first.

items.title: Talked on the pod about the consensus pointing to $24.3 million being too much for Kuminga in a vacuum.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Talked on the pod about the consensus pointing to $24.3 million being too much for Kuminga in a vacuum.

That's backed up by two salary models that are out there:

$9.4M for @johnhollinger.bsky.social's BORD$

$15.3M for @stephnoh.bsky.social

(there is appeal to a 1-year deal, etc.)

items.title: Hollander POTD Jim Spanarkel. Duke SG via Jersey City drafted 16th by PHI in 79. Couldn't get time w/PHI so was taken by DAL in 80 expansion. Got minutes in 80-81 & had 14.4 ppg. PT fell when Ro Blackman came to town & was out of NBA by 84. Has worked TV for CBS & Nets.
